seed_problem: Themed grid network; remove at most k links to maximize the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores achieved delay ratio.
mutation: output: replace the simple 'at most k edges' cardinality budget with a per-edge barricade COST and a total cost budget P (each walkway has both a travel time and a closure price), turning count-interdiction into budgeted shortest-path interdiction -- a knapsack-flavored NP-hard variant where cost-greedy, delay-greedy, and cost/benefit local search diverge.
title: Carnival Circuit Sabotage: Barricading the Ride Walkways
strategies: do-nothing baseline: barricade nothing (achieves the original stroll length, ratio 0.1)
cost-greedy: repeatedly barricade the cheapest affordable walkway lying on the current shortest stroll while keeping the finale reachable
best-improvement local search: at each step tentatively close each affordable shortest-path walkway, recompute the true post-closure shortest stroll, and commit the closure that maximizes it
cost/benefit knapsack heuristic: rank affordable path edges by delay-gain per barricade cost and pack the budget
randomized multi-restart: perturb tie-breaking across restarts and keep the barricade set with the largest final delay
